name: Hooded Acolyte
description: A gaunt figure in dark robes, hunched over ancient texts. His eyes gleam with feverish intensity in the dim light.

start.text: You shouldn't be here. The dead deserve their rest. But since you've come... what do you want?
start.narrative: The acolyte peers at you from beneath his hood, one hand resting protectively on an open book.

responses.text: Tell me about the sealed chamber.
The priestess is worried about you.

studies.text: The Eltharan binding texts. Rituals of containment, seal construction, ward maintenance. Someone must understand them, or we're all doomed when the last seal cracks.
studies.narrative: He turns the ancient pages with trembling fingers.

chamber.text: Below us. Below everything. The sealed chamber holds what the Eltharans couldn't kill. I've felt it stirring — a presence, pressing against the wards. Three seals remain, and the third is cracking.
chamber.narrative: His voice drops to barely a whisper. The candles seem to dim.

fix.text: Theoretically. The seal-stones — fragments of the original binding — are scattered. The forest, the ruins, perhaps the smugglers have some. Gather them, bring them to the chamber, and the ritual might hold. Might.
fix.narrative: He grips your arm with surprising strength.

break.text: Then the shadow wakes. And Northkeep — perhaps more — falls into darkness. The old texts describe it as a hunger that devours light itself. We must not let that happen.
break.narrative: The acolyte's face is pale, his eyes wide with genuine fear.

priestess.text: Sera means well, but she doesn't understand. Reading about the seals from temple scrolls isn't the same as *feeling* them weaken. I'm down here because I must be. Someone has to watch.
priestess.narrative: He softens slightly at the mention of the priestess.

responses.text: Tell me about the sealed chamber.
